## Task: Delete brittle static-asset tests that regex-match source text
## Step: review

Delete five brittle test files/suites that assert on source-text patterns rather than testing behavior. These tests break on formatting changes and offer low regression-protection value:

1. **`src/app.css.test.ts`** (606 lines) — Uses a 280-line custom CSS parser to regex-match CSS source text for 43 rules. The vitest environment does not load CSS, so these tests can never verify actual styling behavior — they only pin the exact source text of app.css, which breaks on any formatting change.

2. **`src/index.html.test.ts`** (230 lines) — Regex-matches the HTML source of `wwwroot/index.html` for specific markup patterns (charset, viewport, title, noscript, script src). These assertions pin exact source formatting rather than behavior.

3. **`src/readme.test.ts`** (250 lines) — Regex-matches README.md source text, asserting specific wordings and substring presence. Creates a maintenance coupling between prose and code.

4. **`TestProject.Tests/ReadmeDocumentationTests.cs`** (241 lines) — Pins exact README wording and magic numbers (500, 32) via source-text substring matching. Creates a temporal coupling between documentation prose and code.

5. **`TestProject.Tests/ProgramWiringTests.cs`** (335 lines) — Regex-matches Program.cs source text to verify DI wiring and middleware ordering. Tests source formatting rather than actual runtime DI behavior.

Delete all five files entirely.

Verification: Run `npm test` (vitest) and `dotnet test TestProject.sln` and confirm all remaining tests pass.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ approved: Whether the implementation is accepted, feedback: Detailed review feedback, issues: Array<{ file: File with the issue, description: What needs to be fixed, severity: "critical" | "minor" /* How important the fix is */ }> }
